prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `b9630600`.
codex_response:
Rule summary: the input is a set of green rectangular room outlines. The output adds the minimum set of shortest orthogonal corridors needed to connect all rooms, but only between pairs whose interior spans overlap on the perpendicular axis; wide corridors stay hollow, narrow ones collapse to solid bars.

I discarded the `arc2_sonnet45_summary.jsonl` hint and only used `arc2_opus46_summary.json` as a starting point. The sonnet hint describes diagonal bridges to filled rectangles, which is inconsistent with the official task; the corrected MST-style room-connection rule is documented in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b9630600/notes.md). The generator stays close to the task family by sampling 6-7 separated room outlines and rendering outputs with the same facing-room corridor geometry. No new primitive was needed.

Task package: [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b9630600/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b9630600/verifier.py), [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b9630600/generator.py), [helpers.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b9630600/helpers.py), [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b9630600/notes.md).

Verification: official `4/4` passed, generated `1000/1000` passed.
